_source: International Plumbing Code 2021, Uniform Plumbing Code, Contractor field manuals
_note: Long-tail plumbing code data. Pipe sizing, venting, fixture units, drain slopes - specific calculations.

water_supply_fixture_units.definition: WSFU = standardized demand unit; accounts for intermittent use

water_supply_sizing_table.explanation: Size based on total WSFU and developed length (actual pipe run including fittings)

pex_tubing.note: PEX has higher friction loss than copper; reduce capacity 10-15% or size up
pex_tubing.1/2_inch_pex: Equivalent to 3/8" copper for long runs; use 3/4" PEX for >50ft runs or >3 fixtures
pex_tubing.manifold_system: Home-run PEX to each fixture; no tees; 1/2" adequate for individual fixtures

water_velocity_limits.reason: Exceeding velocity causes water hammer, erosion, noise
water_velocity_limits.example: 10 GPM through 1/2" (0.625" ID): V = 10.5 fps; too high; use 3/4"

pressure_considerations.minimum_required: 20 PSI at highest fixture; 40-60 PSI typical residential
pressure_considerations.maximum_allowed: 80 PSI code limit; install PRV if street pressure >80 PSI
pressure_considerations.pressure_loss: Approx 5 PSI per 100ft elevation gain; 10 PSI per 100ft horizontal (depends on flow rate and pipe size)

drainage_fixture_units.definition: DFU = drainage demand; 1 DFU = 7.5 gallons/minute (1 cubic foot/minute)

horizontal_drain_sizing.slope_requirement: 1/4" per foot minimum for drains ≤3"; 1/8" per foot for ≥4" drains
horizontal_drain_sizing.max_slope: Code allows vertical (90°), but practical max 45° to prevent solids racing ahead of water

vent_sizing_and_rules.purpose: Allow air into drain system; prevents siphoning trap seals; equalizes pressure
vent_sizing_and_rules.individual_vent: Each fixture; vent size ≥ drain size up to 1.5"; 1.5" vent for 2" drain
vent_sizing_and_rules.wet_vent: Drain pipe also acts as vent for upstream fixtures; limited applications; toilets downstream only
vent_sizing_and_rules.common_vent: Single vent serves two traps (back-to-back sinks); traps at same level
vent_sizing_and_rules.circuit_vent: Vent serves multiple fixtures on horizontal drain; vent at upstream end

vent_distance_limits.exceeding_distance: Trap seal siphoned; vent must be within these distances from trap weir

vent_sizing_and_rules.vent_termination: 6" above roof; 10ft from vertical surfaces; extend through roof (not sidewall vent unless special provisions)

studor_vent_aav.description: Air Admittance Valve; allows air in but not out; one-way valve
studor_vent_aav.code_acceptance: Allowed by IPC; prohibited by some local codes; check jurisdiction
studor_vent_aav.limitations: Cannot use for entire system; must have at least one open-air vent; individual fixture or fixture group only
studor_vent_aav.installation: 6" above flood rim of highest fixture served; accessible location

trap_requirements.each_fixture_trapped: Every fixture must have trap; toilets have integral trap; sinks/tubs use P-trap
trap_requirements.no_double_trapping: Only one trap per fixture; double trap creates air lock
trap_requirements.no_s_traps: Illegal; siphons trap seal; replace with P-trap and proper vent
trap_requirements.trap_arm_slope: 1/4" per foot toward drain; prevents standing water in trap arm
trap_requirements.cleanout_requirements: Within 5ft of base of every vertical stack; horizontal drain direction changes >45°; every 100ft horizontal run

water_supply.copper_type_l: Standard for residential; soldered joints; long life; freeze-sensitive
water_supply.copper_type_m: Thinner wall; cheaper; adequate for residential per code
water_supply.pex: Cross-linked polyethylene; flexible; freeze-tolerant; crimped or cinch clamps; no torch
water_supply.cpvc: Chlorinated PVC; solvent welded; cheaper than copper; brittle in cold; less common now
water_supply.galvanized_steel: Old homes; corrodes; rust reduces flow; replace don't extend

dwv_drain.abs: Black plastic; solvent weld; common in western US
dwv_drain.pvc_schedule_40: White plastic; solvent weld; common nationwide; primer required
dwv_drain.cast_iron: Heavy; quiet; expensive; rubber couplings (no-hub) or oakum/lead joints
dwv_drain.galvanized_steel: Old; corrodes; illegal for new work in most jurisdictions

transition_fittings.copper_to_pex: Brass crimp fitting or push-connect SharkBite
transition_fittings.abs_to_pvc: Green transition cement approved for both
transition_fittings.cast_iron_to_pvc: Fernco rubber coupling; allows dissimilar material connection

sizing.first_hour_rating: Most important; gallons delivered in first hour of heavy use

installation_code.tpr_valve: Temperature/Pressure Relief valve; 210°F or 150 PSI trips; discharge pipe to floor or outdoors
installation_code.discharge_pipe: Same size as valve outlet (3/4"); terminate 6" from floor; no threads on end; no valve in discharge line
installation_code.drain_pan: Required if indoors above living space; 1" drain to safe location
installation_code.seismic_strapping: Two straps; upper 1/3 and lower 1/3 of tank in seismic zones
installation_code.clearances: Gas: 6" from combustibles; electric: none required; follow manual

venting_gas_heaters.natural_draft: Single-wall vent; 1" clearance to combustibles; rise 1/4" per foot minimum
venting_gas_heaters.direct_vent: Sealed combustion; PVC or AL29-4C; intake and exhaust through wall
venting_gas_heaters.power_vent: Fan-assisted; PVC vent; horizontal run allowed; more flexible placement

expansion_tank.purpose: Closed system (with backflow preventer) needs thermal expansion relief
expansion_tank.sizing: 2 gallon tank for 40-50 gallon heater; 5 gallon for 80+ gallon heater
expansion_tank.installation: On cold inlet; pressurize air side to match system pressure (typically 50 PSI)

common_code_violations.vent_too_far: Trap to vent exceeds maximum distance; trap siphons; sewer gas enters home
common_code_violations.improper_slope: Drain slope too shallow (water stands) or too steep (solids left behind)
common_code_violations.no_cleanout: Drain clog inaccessible; must snake from roof; expensive; cleanout required
common_code_violations.s_trap_bootleg: Drain goes down then up then down; air lock; siphons trap seal
common_code_violations.galvanized_to_copper: Direct connection; galvanic corrosion; use dielectric union
common_code_violations.vent_terminates_under_overhang: Sewer gas re-enters home; must extend above roof or 10ft horizontal from windows
